story.title: Bubbletea File Browser

user-story.as-a: photographer using Recipe from the terminal
user-story.i-want: an interactive file browser with keyboard navigation
user-story.so-that: I can visually select preset files for conversion without typing full paths or using shell wildcards

story.business-value:
The Bubbletea-based TUI file browser transforms Recipe from a CLI-only tool into an interactive terminal application that bridges the gap between command-line power and GUI convenience:
- Visual File Management - Browse directories and see file previews without leaving the terminal - Reduced Cognitive Load - No need to remember file paths or glob patterns - Power User Efficiency - Keyboard-driven workflow is faster than mouse-based GUIs for batch operations - Professional Workflow Integration - Terminal users (developers, sys admins, power users) prefer TUI over switching to GUI tools
Strategic value: TUI interface differentiates Recipe from web-only converters and positions it as a professional power-user tool. Photographers working in terminal-heavy workflows (server environments, SSH sessions, automated pipelines) can use Recipe without GUI overhead.

criterion.title: Bubbletea Application Initialization
criterion.description: TUI application initialization and basic lifecycle management

requirements.requirement: TUI application built using Bubbletea framework (github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea/v2)
Application launches with `recipe-tui` command (separate binary from `recipe` CLI)
Initial state shows current working directory
Terminal size detected and UI adapts to terminal dimensions
Application exits cleanly with Ctrl+C or 'q' key

criterion.test-guidance: Test model initialization, verify currentDir starts at ".", confirm terminal width detection, test clean exit handlers

criterion.title: Directory Navigation

requirements.requirement: Display current directory path at top of screen
List files and subdirectories in current directory
Arrow keys navigate up/down through file list
Enter key on directory navigates into that directory
Backspace or left arrow navigates to parent directory
Home/End keys jump to first/last item in list

criterion.test-guidance: Test navigation with nested directories, verify cursor movement, test boundary conditions (first/last item), test parent navigation from deep paths

criterion.title: File Filtering by Format
criterion.description: Display only supported preset formats with metadata

requirements.requirement: Display only supported preset formats: .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate
Show directories (for navigation) but filter non-preset files
Display file extension badges (NP3, XMP, LRT) with color coding
Show file size in human-readable format (KB, MB)
Empty directory message if no preset files found

criterion.test-guidance: Test with mixed file types, verify only presets + directories shown, test empty directory message, validate badge colors

criterion.title: Multi-File Selection
criterion.description: Keyboard-based multi-file selection with visual indicators

requirements.requirement: Space bar toggles selection on current file
Selected files marked with checkmark (✓) indicator
Status bar shows count of selected files
'a' key selects all files in current directory
'n' key deselects all files (clears selection)
Selection state persists across directory navigation

criterion.test-guidance: Test selection toggle, verify checkmark display, test select-all/deselect-all, test selection persistence when navigating directories

criterion.title: File Information Display
criterion.description: Rich file metadata display in UI

requirements.requirement: Display file name, size, and last modified date
Show file path (full absolute path) in status bar when file selected
Display total count of files in current directory
Show format type (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) with color-coded badge

criterion.test-guidance: Verify all metadata fields display correctly, test path truncation for long paths, validate color-coded badges match format

criterion.title: Keyboard Shortcuts Help
criterion.description: In-app help overlay for keyboard shortcuts

requirements.requirement: '?' key displays help overlay with all keyboard shortcuts
Help overlay can be dismissed with '?' or Escape key
Help overlay lists all navigation, selection, and action keys

criterion.test-guidance: Test help toggle (? to show, ? or Esc to hide), verify all shortcuts documented in help, test help overlay styling

criterion.title: Terminal Resize Handling
criterion.description: Responsive UI that adapts to terminal size changes

requirements.requirement: UI adapts to terminal resize events
File list scrolls if more files than terminal height
Layout remains functional at minimum 80x24 terminal size
Help overlay adapts to terminal size

criterion.test-guidance: Test with various terminal sizes, verify scrolling with large file lists, test minimum size constraint, verify resize handling during operation

task-group.name: Task 1: Project Structure Setup
task-group.task: Create `cmd/tui/` directory
Create `cmd/tui/main.go` with basic structure (package main, main() entry point, tea.NewProgram call)

task-group.name: Task 2: Dependency Installation
task-group.task: Install Bubbletea v2 experimental: `go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea/v2@v2-exp`
Install Bubbles v2 experimental: `go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/v2@v2-exp`
Install Lipgloss v2 experimental: `go get github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/v2@v2-exp`
Install Charm X packages: `go get github.com/charmbracelet/x@v0.2.0`

task-group.name: Task 3: Model Definition
task-group.task: Define initial model struct in `cmd/tui/model.go` with fields: currentDir, files, cursor, selected, termWidth, termHeight, showHelp, err
Implement `Init()` method returning loadFiles command
Implement `Update()` method skeleton handling KeyMsg, WindowSizeMsg, fileListMsg
Implement `View()` method skeleton with basic rendering structure

task-group.name: Task 4: File Loading
task-group.task: Create `loadFiles()` command reading directory with os.ReadDir and filtering for .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate
Implement `detectFormat()` helper returning format string ("NP3"/"XMP"/"LRT") based on extension
Define fileInfo struct with fields: name, path, size, modTime, isDir, format

task-group.name: Task 5: Keyboard Navigation
task-group.task: Create `handleKeyPress()` method handling up/down arrow keys, enter, backspace, home, end, q, ctrl+c
Implement `navigateInto()` method for entering directories (updates currentDir, reloads files, resets cursor)
Implement `navigateUp()` method for parent directory navigation (filepath.Dir, reloads files)

task-group.name: Task 6: Multi-File Selection
task-group.task: Add selection toggle to `handleKeyPress()` for space, 'a', 'n' keys
Implement `toggleSelection()` adding/removing current file from selected map
Implement `selectAll()` and `deselectAll()` methods for bulk selection operations

task-group.name: Task 7: Lipgloss Styling
task-group.task: Create `renderFileList()` using Lipgloss for styled file list rendering
Implement `renderFileLine()` with cursor highlight, selection indicator, badge, size formatting
Implement `formatBadge()` returning color-coded format badges using Lipgloss styles
Implement `formatSize()` converting bytes to human-readable KB/MB format

task-group.name: Task 8: Help Overlay
task-group.task: Create `renderHelp()` returning formatted help text with keyboard shortcuts table
Update `handleKeyPress()` to handle '?' and Escape keys for help toggle

task-group.name: Task 9: Terminal Resize Handling
task-group.task: Update `Update()` to handle WindowSizeMsg and update termWidth/termHeight in model
Implement scrolling for long file lists (track viewport offset, render visible slice)

task-group.name: Task 10: Polish and UX
task-group.task: Add 'r' key handler for manual refresh/reload
Show refresh indicator during file load operations

task-group.name: Task 11: Testing
task-group.task: Create `cmd/tui/model_test.go` with unit tests for navigation, selection, file filtering
Test coverage goal: >85% for model logic

task-group.name: Task 12: Build and Documentation
task-group.task: Update `Makefile` adding `recipe-tui` build target
Add TUI to GitHub Actions CI (go build, go test)
Update `README.md` with TUI usage section
Create `docs/tui-guide.md` with screenshots (ASCII art) and workflow examples

documentation.doc: Contains functional requirements FR-4.1 (Interactive File Browser), FR-4.2 (Live Parameter Preview), FR-4.3 (Batch Progress), FR-4.4 (Visual Validation). This story implements FR-4.1 specifically.
Describes Recipe as hybrid web app + CLI tool with three interfaces: CLI, TUI, Web. TUI targets power users with interactive workflows.
Documents Bubbletea framework selection rationale, Elm Architecture pattern (Model-Update-View), cross-platform terminal support, and integration with Lipgloss styling.
Shows cmd/{cli,tui,wasm}/ + internal/ layout. TUI lives in cmd/tui/ and uses shared internal/converter packages.

related-stories.story: Future story that will add parameter preview pane showing extracted preset data when file selected in browser.
Future story that will add conversion progress UI for selected files with real-time status updates.
Future story that will add preview/confirmation screen before executing batch conversion operations.

sample.description: 22 NP3 sample files for testing format detection and file filtering
913 XMP sample files for testing format detection and file filtering
544 lrtemplate sample files for testing format detection and file filtering
Additional XMP test files for validation

existing-code.note: This is the first story in Epic 4. No existing TUI code exists yet. Story 4-1 creates the foundation for future TUI stories.

reference.description: Future stories (4-2+) will integrate with converter package for parameter extraction and conversion operations
UniversalRecipe model will be used by Story 4-2 for live parameter preview

external-packages.package:
Core TUI framework implementing Elm Architecture (Model-Update-View pattern). Handles terminal events, rendering, and application lifecycle.
CRITICAL: v2 experimental version with breaking changes from v1: - Import path: github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea/v2 (note /v2 suffix) - Install: go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea/v2@v2-exp - Migration guide: https://github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea/tree/v2-exp

Reusable TUI components (file picker, text input, progress bar, viewport). Future stories will use these components.
CRITICAL: v2 experimental version with breaking changes from v1: - Import path: github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/v2 (note /v2 suffix) - Install: go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/v2@v2-exp - Migration guide: https://github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/tree/v2-exp

Terminal styling library providing CSS-like styling for TUI. Used for colors, borders, padding, and layout.
CRITICAL: v2 experimental version with breaking changes from v1: - Import path: github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/v2 (note /v2 suffix) - Install: go get github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/v2@v2-exp - Migration guide: https://github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss/tree/v2-exp
Charm experimental packages providing additional utilities (ansi, input, term helpers). Install: go get github.com/charmbracelet/x@v0.2.0

internal-packages.note: Story 4-1 has zero internal dependencies - it's the foundation for Epic 4.

future-dependency.usage: Parameter extraction for live preview pane

technical.constraint: Must use Bubbletea Elm Architecture (Model-Update-View) - state immutability, pure functions for Update/View
Must work on Windows, macOS, Linux terminals with minimum 80x24 size
File listing should handle directories with 1000+ files without lag (use lazy loading/pagination if needed)
100% keyboard-driven - no mouse support required (Bubbletea supports mouse but not needed for this story)
CRITICAL - Use v2 experimental branches with /v2 import paths. Code will NOT work with v1 versions.

architectural.constraint: TUI lives in cmd/tui/ - separate binary from cmd/cli/main.go (recipe) and cmd/wasm/main.go
File format detection logic should be extracted to helper functions for reuse in future stories
All UI state stored in model struct - no global variables (Bubbletea requirement)
Model logic must be testable - separate business logic from rendering where possible

ui-ux.constraint: UI must update within 16ms (60fps) for smooth user experience
Clear visual indicators for selection, cursor position, and keyboard shortcuts
Graceful error messages for permission denied, directory not found, etc.
Color scheme and styling should be consistent across all TUI screens (use shared Lipgloss styles)

testing.constraint: Minimum 85% test coverage for model logic (navigation, selection, filtering)
Must manually test on Windows PowerShell, macOS Terminal, and Linux terminal emulators
Test with empty directories, single file, 1000+ files, deeply nested paths

interface.keys: Enter: Navigate into directory or select file
Backspace/Left Arrow: Navigate to parent directory
a: Select all files in current directory

interface.section: File List: Scrollable list with cursor, selection indicators, badges, sizes
Status Bar: Selected file count, total file count, current file path
Help Overlay: Keyboard shortcuts reference (modal overlay)

interface.state-field: files: Array of fileInfo structs (filtered preset files + directories)
selected: Map of selected file paths (for multi-select persistence)
termWidth: Current terminal width (updated on resize)
termHeight: Current terminal height (updated on resize)
showHelp: Boolean flag for help overlay visibility
err: Error state for displaying error messages

unit-tests.test-area: Model Initialization: Verify model initializes with correct default values (currentDir=".", cursor=0, selected empty)
Navigation Logic: Test cursor movement (up/down/home/end), directory navigation (enter/backspace), boundary conditions
Selection Logic: Test toggle selection, select all, deselect all, selection persistence across navigation
File Filtering: Test only .np3/.xmp/.lrtemplate files shown (plus directories), test empty directory handling
Format Detection: Test detectFormat() returns correct format strings for each extension
Error Handling: Test handling of permission errors, invalid paths, missing directories

integration-tests.test-area: Real File System: Test with actual example files from examples/ and testdata/ directories
Large Directories: Test performance and scrolling with 100+ file directories
Deep Paths: Test navigation through deeply nested directory structures

manual-tests.test-area: Cross-Platform: Test on Windows (PowerShell, CMD), macOS (Terminal.app, iTerm2), Linux (gnome-terminal, xterm)
Terminal Sizes: Test at 80x24 (minimum), 120x40 (typical), 200x60 (large), resize during operation
Keyboard Layouts: Test with different keyboard layouts (QWERTY, AZERTY, etc.)
Visual Inspection: Verify colors, alignment, spacing, cursor visibility, selection indicators

technical-debt.debt-item: v2 experimental versions are pre-release and may have breaking changes before stable v2 release. Monitor upstream for changes and be prepared to update code.
No pagination/lazy loading in Story 4-1 - large directories (1000+ files) will load all into memory. Consider adding viewport/pagination in future performance optimization story if needed.
File metadata caching not implemented - directory reload fetches all file stats again. Could optimize with caching layer in future if performance becomes issue.

validation.step: Run `go build ./cmd/tui` to verify TUI builds successfully
Run `go test ./cmd/tui -v` to verify all tests pass with >85% coverage
Manual test: Launch TUI and verify file browser functionality on target platform
Manual test: Verify keyboard shortcuts work as documented in help overlay
Manual test: Test with large directory from examples/ to verify performance
